article.title: Apple Turns 50 With Global Concert Series, Paul McCartney Finale, and an Animated Homepage Tribute to Five Decades of Products
article.summary: Apple marks its 50th anniversary on April 1, 2026, with worldwide celebrations spanning 11 countries, a Paul McCartney concert at Apple Park, and a homepage animation tracing the company's product history from the original Macintosh to Vision Pro.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Apple turned 50 on April 1, 2026, exactly half a century after Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak founded the company in the Jobs family garage in Los Altos, California. The milestone prompted the company's most elaborate public celebration in years: a multi-week, multi-continent concert and community event series that culminated in a private Paul McCartney performance for employees at Apple Park and an animated homepage tribute to five decades of product design.

## What We Know

**Global event series across 11 countries**

Apple staged celebrations at flagship stores and cultural venues around the world throughout March, according to [Apple's newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-hosts-50th-anniversary-celebrations-around-the-world/). The series opened on March 13 with a live performance by 17-time Grammy Award winner Alicia Keys at Apple Grand Central in New York City, where she played from the store's iconic central staircase. Subsequent events included Mumford & Sons and DJ Nia Archives at Apple Battersea in London; fashion designer Feng Chen Wang staging a show themed "Life and Love" during Shanghai Fashion Week at Apple Jing'an; pop and electronic artist Chris Lee (Li Yuchun) performing at Apple Taikoo Li in Chengdu; visual artist Mira Felicia Malhotra unveiling an iPad-created mural at Apple BKC in Mumbai; and virtual artist Mori Calliope performing at Apple Omotesando in Tokyo.

Further stops included Vancouver, where figure skater Elladj Balde led a Today at Apple session on his iPhone-shot "Wild Ice" series; Mexico City, where a filmmaker panel discussed Apple TV+ productions including "Las Azules" and "Acapulco"; and Washington, D.C., where Academy Award winner Troy Kotsur joined disability rights advocate Haben Girma and Gallaudet University president Roberta Cordano for a session on Deaf creativity and accessibility, as reported by [Apple](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-hosts-50th-anniversary-celebrations-around-the-world/). In Sydney, Apple collaborated with the Sydney Opera House on "Illuminating Creativity," a project featuring digital artwork by 11 emerging Australian artists projected onto the Bennelong sails.

**Paul McCartney headlines Apple Park finale**

The celebration concluded on April 1 with a private concert at Apple Park in Cupertino, where Paul McCartney performed a 25-song set spanning Beatles and solo material for Apple employees, according to [TechRadar's live coverage](https://www.techradar.com/news/live/apple-50th-anniversary-celebration). Tim Cook introduced McCartney by noting that Steve Jobs "loved The Beatles" and "often said he built Apple to be like them." The Apple Park campus featured a large 50th anniversary logo statue in Apple's classic rainbow color scheme, alongside displays of every iMac generation from the G3 through M4 models and every iPhone from the original through iPhone 17 Pro Max and iPhone Air, as [TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/news/live/apple-50th-anniversary-celebration) reported. Tim Cook also rang the NASDAQ opening bell from Apple Park on March 31.

**Animated homepage and employee gifts**

Apple replaced its homepage on April 1 with an animated video rendered in a sketch art illustrative style, tracing the company's product evolution from the original Macintosh through the iMac, iPod, App Store, Apple Watch, iPhone 17 Pro, and Vision Pro, as reported by [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/01/apple-50-birthday-homepage-celebration/). The page displayed the message: "50 Years of Thinking Different. At 50 years, it's only natural to look back. But Apple has always looked forward, building tools and delivering experiences that enrich people's lives."

Employees received a commemorative gift package consisting of a t-shirt, enamel pin, and limited-edition poster, all featuring the scribble-style rainbow Apple logo that served as the anniversary's visual identity, according to [TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/news/live/apple-50th-anniversary-celebration). The items were available for pickup at Apple Park through April 30.

**Tim Cook's reflections**

In an open letter published on Apple's website, Cook wrote that "thinking different has always been at the heart of Apple" and credited the company's success to its philosophy of combining technology with liberal arts and human-centered design, according to [Apple's newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/03/apple-to-celebrate-50-years-of-thinking-different/). He pledged continued commitments to environmental responsibility, education, and community impact. On social media, Cook posted: "50 years of Apple, 50 years of innovation. Thank you to our teams, our users, and everyone who's been part of the journey," as [TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/news/live/apple-50th-anniversary-celebration) reported.

## What We Don't Know

Apple has not indicated whether the 50th anniversary will carry forward into any product launches, limited-edition hardware, or other commercial tie-ins beyond the employee memorabilia and store events. The company also has not disclosed how many people attended the global event series or whether any of the Apple Park concert footage will be released publicly. Whether the animated homepage tribute will remain accessible as a permanent archive or revert to standard marketing content has not been stated.
